Notice Of Hearing On Uncontested Petition For Approval Of Minor'S Employment Contract
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition for Approval of the Employment Contract for the Minor, Alaya High.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Elliot P. Cahn, Esq., David M. Given, Esq. and Tucker Page, Esq. appeared for petitioner Empire Distribution, Inc.
Joseph Seiler, Director of Business Affairs for petitioner Empire Distribution, Inc., was placed under oath and testified.
Nicole Lynn, Esq. (counsel for the minor Alaya Hgh), the minor, Alaya High, and her Guardian ad litem, Acie High, appeared via CourtCall.
Mr. High and the minor were placed under oath and testified.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

(1) The Contract is approved as to all of the terms and conditions set forth therein;

(2) Petitioner Acie High is ORDERED to deduct, until the minor attains the age of eighteen years, fifteen percent (15%) of the gross earnings payable by petitioner to and/or for the services of the minor pursuant to the Contract, and to deposit said fifteen percent (15%) into one or more Coogan Trust Accounts establised and maintained for the benefit of the minor at one or more financial institutions in the State of Georgia in accordance with California Family Code Section 6753; and

(3) Except as provided in California Family Code Section 6753 (as amended effective January 1, 2004), there shall be no withdrawals of funds on deposit in said Coogan Trust Accounts to minor Alaya High until she (Alaya High) attains the age of eighteen (18) years on January 28, 2025, or until further ORDER of this Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the establishment and funding the Coogan Trust Accounts.

Proposed Order Approving the Contract of the Minor was submitted to and signed by the Court.
